topic.name: Oil and Gas Regulation
topic.description: Legislation governing oil and gas leases, royalties, and environmental protections related to offshore drilling.

Document 1: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to declare that any royalty under an oil and gas lease on submerged lands of the outer Continental Shelf shall apply to all oil that is removed under the lease and discharged into waters of the United States or ocean waters.
Increases the minimum royalty under such leases from 12.5% to 20%.

topic.name: Tax Reform and Incentives
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at reforming the tax code and providing tax incentives across different sectors including healthcare, education, and energy.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the taxation of individual and married taxpayers
increase from $1,000 to $2,500 the child tax credit
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to require employers to include in employee wage statements the total amount paid for coverage under an accident or health plan
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal the $2,500 limitation on salary reduction contributions to health flexible spending arrangements
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to reduce from 35% to 23% the maximum income tax rate for corporations
Document 8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow employees of prekindergarten, elementary, and secondary schools a tax credit of up to $3,000

topic.name: Education Policy and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts aimed at enhancing education through financial support, safety measures, and professional development for educators.

Document 1: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to cancel the Perkins loans of full-time federal, state, and local fire fighters.
Document 2: Establish a Pediatric Early Literacy program under which the Secretary of Education awards matching grants to nonprofit organizations.
Document 3: Amends title II of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to include teacher and principal training in practices that address the social and emotional development needs of students.
Document 4: Direct the Secretary of Education to transfer funds from other programs to the Impact Aid program.
Document 5: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to allow the placement and use of surveillance equipment in schools.
Document 6: Direct the Department of Education (ED) to award competitive, renewable grants to local educational agencies (LEAs), states, and partnerships for the development and implementation of innovative teacher retention programs.
Document 7: Require each institution of higher education to include in its annual security report a statement of policy regarding harassment.
Document 8: Modify provisions of the teacher loan forgiveness program to reduce the service period for eligibility.
Document 9: Authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to states to integrate financial literacy education into their public middle and secondary schools.
Document 10: Direct the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to support school innovations.

topic.name: Medicare Legislative Amendments
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative amendments to the Medicare program aimed at improving access to healthcare services and benefits for beneficiaries.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish a national minimum payment amount for CPT code 77080
Directs the Secretary to arrange with the Institute of Medicine of the National Academies to conduct a study for a report to the Secretary and Congress
Document 2: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to provide payment under Medicare part A for anesthesia services furnished by a physician who is an anesthesiologist in certain rural hospitals
in the same manner as payment is made for anesthesia services furnished by a certified registered nurse anesthetist
Document 3: This bill amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to direct the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid to report annually to specified congressional committees on Medicare Advantage enrollment data.
Document 4: Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to direct any prescription drug plan that has in its network one or more pharmacies located in a health professional shortage area
to extend to any pharmacy located in that area the option to be an in-network pharmacy
Document 5: Amends part B (Supplementary Medical Insurance) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to limit the penalty for late enrollment under part B to 10%
Provides for a special enrollment period for individuals whose COBRA or retiree coverage terminates.
Document 6: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to remove the restriction that a clinical psychologist or clinical social worker provide services in a comprehensive outpatient rehabilitation facility only under the care of a physician.
Document 7: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to repeal the 96-hour physician certification requirement for inpatient critical access hospital services
a physician must certify that a patient may reasonably be expected to be discharged or transferred to a hospital within 96 hours after admission.
Document 8: Amends part B (Supplementary Medical Insurance) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to revise the Medicare durable medical equipment face-to-face encounter documentation requirement.
Document 9: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to require Medicaid coverage of medical and surgical services of optometrists that are otherwise covered when furnished by a physician.
Document 10: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to revise requirements for determining the relative value of physicians' services for fee payment purposes.
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to provide for a 4-year transition applicable in the case of codes for services for which a single code is used for payment.

topic.name: Tariff Duty Suspensions
topic.description: This cluster focuses on amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding the temporary suspension or adjustment of duties on various goods.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on ethene, tetrafluoro, oxidized, polymerized reduced, methyl esters, reduced.
temporary suspension of duty
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on woven fabrics containing 85 percent or more by weight of vicuna hair.
temporary suspension of duty
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on women's belts of leather or composition leather, each valued $7.00 or higher.
suspend temporarily the duty
Document 4: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the duty suspension on Benzoic acid, 3,4,5-trihydroxy-, propyl ester.
extend the duty suspension
Document 5: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to increase, through December 31, 2011, the duty on Propanoic acid, 2-[4-(cyano-2-fluorophenoxy)phenoxy]-butyl ester (2R) (Cyhalofop).
increase the duty
Document 6: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain mixtures containing Y3-Y(6--chloro-3-pridinyl)methyl?-2-thiazolidinylidene?cyana ide.
suspend temporarily the duty
Document 7: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on self contained, carafe-less automatic drip coffeemaker.
temporary suspension of duty
Document 8: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on ethene, tetrafluoro, oxidized, polymerized reduced, methyl esters, reduced.
temporary suspension of duty
Document 9: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain women's leather or composition leather upper footwear.
suspend temporarily the duty
Document 10: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on small particle calcium chloride phosphate phosphor.
temporary suspension of duty

topic.name: Veterans' Services and Benefits
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving services, benefits, and support for veterans and their families.

Document 1: Amends the Older Americans Act of 1965 to include status as a veteran among the categories of greatest social need.
Requires state and area agencies on aging to engage in outreach to veterans.
Document 2: Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 to make part-time workers eligible for coverage.
Repeals the requirement of 1,250 hours worked for leave eligibility.
Document 3: Increases rates of veterans' benefits for wartime disability compensation.
Makes disabled veterans with severe burn injuries eligible for assistance.
Document 4: Requires the VA to furnish records pertaining to a claim within 14 days.
Informs requesters of delays in record provision.
Document 5: Directs DOD and VA to establish a pilot program for TBI and PTSD treatments.
Requires notification to veterans about treatment opportunities.
Document 6: Entitles family members of deployed Armed Forces members to two workweeks of leave.
Provides employment protection for employees returning from leave.
Document 7: Includes veterans with serious illnesses for caregiver assistance programs.
Requires annual assessments for primary caregivers.
Document 8: Directs VA to provide outreach and support services to veterans in higher education.
Employs veterans to assist peers in using educational assistance.
Document 9: Authorizes VA to contract with community mental health centers for veterans' services.
Document 10: Establishes programs for mental health and readjustment counseling for veterans.
Extends eligibility for care services for veterans returning from combat.

topic.name: Child Protection and Welfare
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at enhancing the protection and welfare of children through various initiatives and funding for prevention, reporting, and legal assistance.

Document 1: Amends the Housing and Community Development Act to revise requirements for activities eligible for community development block grant assistance.
Increases the limitation on the amount of CDBG assistance that may be used to provide public services.
Document 2: Amends the Missing Children's Assistance Act to require the Office of Juvenile Justice and Delinquency Prevention's annual grant to be used to operate a cyber tipline for reporting child sexual exploitation.
Requires states to provide recent photographs of missing children and maintain liaison with child welfare systems.
Document 4: Extends authority for issuing administrative subpoenas in cases involving sexual exploitation or abuse of children to multiple federal agencies.
Document 5: Increases criminal penalties for offenses relating to the sexual exploitation of children and child pornography.
Requires the Secretary of State to deny passports for individuals convicted of sex offenses against children.
Document 6: Amends the Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act to require states to enforce laws against failing to report child abuse or neglect.
Mandates that state plans for child abuse prevention contain assurances about reporting procedures.
Document 7: Directs HHS to make grants for educational campaigns on mandatory reporting of child abuse or neglect.
Establishes state responsibilities for investigating reports of child abuse or neglect.
Document 8: Authorizes grants to create a National Domestic Violence Volunteer Attorney Network to assist victims of domestic violence.
Requires the Attorney General to study legal representation for victims of domestic violence.
Document 9: Adds bullying and gang prevention to the activities supported under the Safe and Drug-Free Schools and Communities program.
Facilitates healthy, physically active lifestyles in educational settings.
Document 10: Amends the Violence Against Women Act to improve definitions and training for handling cases of domestic and sexual violence.
Ensures entities providing training under the Violence Against Women Act have demonstrated expertise.

topic.name: Consumer Financial Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at protecting consumers in financial transactions and improving access to financial services.

Document 1: prohibit predispute arbitration agreements with consumers
valid or enforceable in disputes related to credit card accounts
Document 2: authorize covered financial institutions to conduct a contest
excludes such a 'savings promotion raffle' from the prohibition against a covered financial institution's dealing in 'lottery' tickets
Document 3: redefine the standard maximum deposit insurance amount
authorize the FDIC to impose special assessments
Document 4: create a safe harbor from lawsuit for creditors
treat such a loan as a qualified mortgage
Document 5: encourages the states to establish a Nationwide Mortgage Licensing System
sets forth state license and registration procedures
Document 6: require the Federal National Mortgage Association to disclose promptly
amount by which the purchase price was less than the minimum purchase price
Document 7: prohibit an insured depository institution from being or becoming an affiliate of any insurance company
limits the business of national banks to receiving deposits
Document 8: prohibits a consumer credit card account from being terminated for inactivity
requires the card issuer to investigate whether such an error occurred
Document 9: prohibit a creditor from using any adverse information
clearly and conspicuously described to the consumer by the credit card issuer
Document 10: amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to the presumption of a mortgagor's ability to pay
require private deposit insurers of credit unions to give the Federal Housing Finance Agency copies of independent audit reports

topic.name: Land Conveyance and Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the conveyance, management, and use of federal and tribal lands across various states.

Document 1: conveyance of approximately 44 acres of federally owned land
requires the city to pay the market value of the property
Document 2: Directs the Department of the Interior to take into trust
prohibits the Tribe from conducting gaming on such land
Document 3: directs the Department of Agriculture to accept the offer
the USDA shall determine the values of the lands
Document 6: directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to the Association
requires the completion of such conveyance within 30 days
Document 8: requires a border of any parcel of land that is transferred
authorizes the Navajo Tribe to designate up to 150,000 acres
Document 9: directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey to the state of California
requires the state to cover the costs to be incurred by the Secretary

topic.name: Campaign Finance Transparency
topic.description: The documents focus on enhancing transparency and accountability in campaign finance and lobbying activities in the United States.

1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more to the Federal Election Commission (FEC).
Requires Senate candidates to file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC.
2: This bill amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more.
A report shall now be made only to the FEC.
3: Prohibits conversion of leadership PAC funds to personal use.
Prohibits payment of campaign funds, except nominal reimbursements, to immediate family members of candidates.
5: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise requirements for disclosure of bundled contributions.
Increases the applicable threshold triggering such reporting requirement from $15,000 to $50,000 in the case of a presidential candidate committee.
7: This bill amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require any candidate of a major party for the office of President to file with the Federal Election Commission (FEC) a copy of the candidate's income tax returns.
Shall be treated in the same manner as a report filed by the candidate and shall be made publicly available.
9: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to prohibit any authorized committee of a candidate from employing the spouse or any immediate family member of such candidate.
Subject to specified criminal and civil penalties any spouse of a Member of Congress who knowingly lobbies any Member of Congress.

topic.name: Immigration Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures and acts related to immigration enforcement, eligibility, and the rights of immigrants and refugees in the United States.

Document 1: Revises immigration- and alien-related provisions regarding: alien smuggling and related offenses, identity security, birthright citizenship, housing and homeownership, taxes and social security, employee verification, financial services, border and law enforcement, state and local government authority, and in-state tuition.
Document 2: Prohibits the use of community oriented policing services funds in contravention of provisions of the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996.
Document 3: Prohibits a person from receiving a federal public benefit unless such person is in 'satisfactory immigration status' in accordance with the Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlements (SAVE) program.
Document 4: Amends the federal criminal code to modify the eligibility of certain foreign nationals to purchase and possess firearms in the United States.
Document 6: Prohibits a state or local government from receiving federal financial assistance if it restricts or prohibits a government entity or official from sending to or receiving from the responsible federal immigration agency information regarding an individual's citizenship or immigration status.
Document 9: Requires each applicant for a single-family home mortgage to provide the lender information to verify the applicant's citizenship or alien status through the E-Verify program.

topic.name: Intelligence Community Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and actions related to the oversight, accountability, and operational guidelines of the U.S. intelligence community.

Document 1: Amends the National Security Act of 1947 to direct the head of an element of the intelligence community to provide intelligence information.
Requires the Secretary to report annually to Congress concerning the instances in which such information was provided.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to prepare an annual assessment for the intelligence community's use of private contractors.
Prohibits the use of private contractors for the arrest, interrogation, detention, or transportation of persons under government custody.
Document 5: Requires the Secretary of Defense, the Director of National Intelligence, and the Attorney General to jointly submit to Congress procedures for the interrogation of unprivileged enemy belligerents.
Requires the DNI to notify the congressional intelligence committees semiannually of the identities of any unprivileged enemy belligerents who have been interrogated.
Document 7: Authorizes appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of various intelligence agencies.
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel levels are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.

topic.name: Drug and Food Safety Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving safety, labeling, and regulation of drugs and food products.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to allow the holder of an approved abbreviated new drug application to change the labeling of a drug.
Allows conforming changes to be ordered to the labeling of the equivalent listed drug.
Document 2: Prohibits a person from shipping, transporting, offering for sale, selling, or purchasing a covered fish in interstate commerce.
Defines 'covered fish' as any finfish modified or produced through the application of recombinant DNA technologies.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to extend the exclusivity period for a new prescription drug.
Defines 'qualified infectious disease product' to mean an antibiotic drug for treating, detecting, preventing, or identifying a qualifying pathogen.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to prescribe new drug application requirements for tamper-resistant drugs.
Requires an abbreviated new drug application for a tamper-resistant drug to include testing information.
Document 5: Prohibits any entity from conducting or contracting for cosmetic animal testing in the United States.
Effective on the date that is one year after enactment of this Act.
Document 6: Addresses prescription opioid misuse through various regulatory measures.
Amends the Controlled Substances Act by addressing annual narcotic manufacturing quotas.
Document 7: Directs the FDA to promulgate final regulations establishing tolerances under the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to limit the quantity of total arsenic and lead in beverages containing fruit juice.
Requires the limitations on total arsenic and lead to be no less stringent than allowable levels in bottled water.
Document 8: Directs the Commissioner of Food and Drugs to promulgate a final regulation revising federal regulations applicable to the declaration of the trans fat content on a food label.
Requires trans fat content to be expressed with an asterisk or notation.
Document 9: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to provide that a food shall be deemed to be misbranded if it contains any spice, flavoring, or coloring derived from animal products unless appropriately labeled.
Ensures transparency in food labeling.
Document 10: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to prohibit the license holder of an FDA-approved drug from restricting availability for testing by a product developer.
The FDA may authorize a product developer to conduct testing and clinical trials with the medication.

topic.name: Mental Health Services Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative efforts aimed at improving mental health services and support for individuals, particularly veterans, youth, and families affected by mental health issues.

Document 1: Mental Health Care Provider Retention Act of 2016
allows individuals transitioning from DOD to VA to continue treatment with their DOD mental health provider.
Document 2: Coming Together for National Guard and Reserve Families Act of 2007
expand and enhance access to mental health services for families of members who are disabled during military service.
Document 4: Youth Mental Health Research Act
establish a Youth Mental Health Research Network for research and intervention services.
Document 5: Community Assistance Act for Persons with Mental Illness
provide housing counseling and financial counseling for individuals before their release from institutions.
Document 6: Services, Education, and Rehabilitation for Veterans Act
develop, implement, or enhance veteran's treatment courts for veterans with mental health problems.
Document 7: Helping Families in Mental Health Crisis Act of 2013
establish a National Mental Health Policy Laboratory to improve mental health services.
Document 9: Juvenile Crime Reduction Act
address the service needs of juveniles with mental health or substance abuse disorders.

topic.name: Agricultural Support and Housing Assistance
topic.description: Legislation focused on providing financial assistance and support for agricultural producers and housing programs for vulnerable populations.

Document 1: emergency financial assistance to producers on a farm that incurred qualifying natural disaster-caused quantity or quality losses
grants to state departments of agriculture for assistance to eligible catfish producers
Document 2: emergency relief grants to address the unmet needs of homeless populations
funding to HUD to expand the Healthcare and Housing Systems Integration Initiative
Document 3: suspend the limitation on the period for which borrowers are eligible for loan guarantee assistance
Document 4: extend the requirements for consideration as rural areas with respect to assistance for farm housing
Document 5: extend and revise the counter-cyclical payment program
payments available in a state for a covered commodity
Document 6: establish an urban agriculture outreach program to award grants to eligible entities
expand the Farmer's Market Promotion Program by adding loans and loan guarantees
Document 7: extend assistance to disadvantaged or beginning farmers or ranchers
provide assistance for agricultural rehabilitation and vocational training for veterans
Document 8: extend federal procurement of biobased products
establish a biochar research and demonstration program
Document 9: provide cost-share payments to agricultural producers for water conservation projects
Document 10: make biorefinery assistance available for renewable chemicals projects

topic.name: U.S. Nuclear and Sanctions Policy
topic.description: The documents collectively address U.S. legislative actions regarding nuclear policy, arms control, and economic sanctions primarily focused on Iran and Russia.

Document 1: Directs the President to conduct a nuclear policy review
Requires the Secretary of Defense to conduct a comprehensive review of the U.S. nuclear posture
Document 2: Expresses the sense of Congress that the Russian Federation is in material breach of its Intermediate-Range Nuclear Forces (INF) Treaty obligations
Directs the President to establish a program to develop certain ground-launched cruise missile and ground-launched ballistic missile capabilities
Document 3: Directs the President to block all property and property interest transactions of an entity that is U.S-located or controlled by a U.S. person if it is at least 20% owned by Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps

The Iran Threat Reduction and Syria Human Rights Act of 2012 is amended to express the sense of Congress that the United States should support any state or local government that divests from IRGC-related companies
Document 4: Amends the Venezuela Defense of Human Rights and Civil Society Act of 2014 to extend provisions requiring the President to impose sanctions against individuals in Venezuela who are responsible for human rights violations
Document 5: Requires the President to block and prohibit all transactions in property and property interests of a foreign person that knowingly supports Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps
The President shall review an identified foreign person to determine whether there is sufficient evidence to impose sanctions
Document 6: Sets forth U.S. policy regarding normalization of relations with Iran
Directs the President to reduce the dollar threshold for imposition of sanctions against persons making investments that contribute to Iran's petroleum resource development
Document 7: Declares that nothing in this Act shall be construed to authorize the use force against Iran
Subjects Iran to certain economic sanctions
Document 8: States that it is U.S. policy to prevent the Islamic Republic of Iran from acquiring a nuclear weapons capability
Requires the President to respond within 60 days to a congressional request regarding a person's engagement in a sanctionable activity
Document 9: Directs the Secretary of State to provide humanitarian assistance to vulnerable populations in Iraq and Iraqi refugees in neighboring countries
Increases refugee admission limitations for the purpose of admitting Iraqi citizens or nationals
Document 10: Recognizes that Christians and Yazidis in Iraq, Syria, Pakistan, Iran, Egypt, and Libya are targets of genocide
The Department of Homeland Security shall provide for expedited visa processing for refugees from these regions.

topic.name: Social Security Act Amendments
topic.description: Legislative amendments related to the Social Security Act addressing eligibility, benefits, and restrictions for various groups including non-citizens and veterans.

Document 1: Prohibit the Commissioner of Social Security from assigning a Social Security account number to any individual who...
Prohibits the payment of OASDI benefits to such individuals.
Document 2: Prohibit issuance of Social Security numbers to nonimmigrant aliens who are admitted to the United States as students...
Document 4: Exclude from creditable wages and self-employment income any wages earned for services by aliens...
Document 5: Treat as a disability for OASDI and SSI disability payment purposes any service-connected total disability...
Document 6: Eliminate the five-month waiting period for an individual... to be eligible for benefits based on disability.
Document 7: Revise the formula for the computation of minimum Old Age Insurance benefits for individuals who reached age 65...
Document 9: Amends title II... with respect to the determination of a valid marriage to account for marriages in jurisdictions other than a state.
Document 10: Extend the $250 economic recovery payments to recipients of Social Security, Supplemental Security Income...

topic.name: Transportation Safety and Standards
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing safety and operational standards across various modes of transportation.

Document 1: Requires each automobile manufacturer's annual covered inventory to comprise at least: (1) 50% fuel choice-enabling vehicles in model years 2015-2017
Defines 'fuel choice-enabling vehicle' to mean an automobile warranted by its manufacturer to be capable of operating on: (1) an advanced alternative fuel blend
Document 2: Requires each air carrier, in the case of a delayed departure, to provide passengers with: (1) adequate food, potable water, and restroom facilities
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to issue regulations to allow natural gas vehicles to exceed any federal weight limitations
Document 5: Reauthorizes appropriations to the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) for FY2015-FY2020 to carry out rail safety activities for the safe transportation of hazardous materials
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to modify certain federal regulations to: (1) allow fire services personnel to wear high visibility apparel meeting certain requirements
Document 7: Directs the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security (Assistant Secretary) (Transportation Security Administration [TSA]) to prescribe employment standards for air carrier personnel and airport security personnel
Document 9: This bill modifies the approval process used for certain transportation projects on Indian reservations by allowing categorical exclusions from National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) requirements
Document 10: Authorizes the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to provide grants to states to repair or replace bridges the Federal Highway Administration has found to be structurally deficient

topic.name: Fishery Management Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management, conservation, and economic support of fisheries in the United States under the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act.

Document 1: Rhode Island to the Mid-Atlantic Fishery Management Council
increase Council membership from 21 to 23 members.
Document 2: require a yearly update of each fishery impact statement
minimize adverse economic impacts on such communities.
Document 3: appropriates funds for assistance for the economic and social effects of the commercial fishery failure
designated under specified provisions of the Magnuson-Stevens Act.
Document 4: directs the Mid-Atlantic Fishery Management Council to submit for approval a modified fishery management plan
based on the best scientific information available.
Document 5: postpone the effective date upon which a mechanism for specifying annual catch limits must be established
for fisheries other than those determined to be subject to overfishing.
Document 6: revise requirements on fishing and fisheries in the Gulf of Mexico
expand state jurisdiction over fisheries in the Gulf.
Document 7: establishes an economic assistance program to assist fishing communities
significantly affected by a prohibition on the retention of stocks.
Document 8: conduct a benchmark stock assessment of the South Atlantic red snapper fishery
prohibits fishing in the South Atlantic red snapper fishery before such report.
Document 9: require the Secretary of Commerce to annually make available funding for Council research
from violations within the Council's fishery management jurisdiction.
Document 10: add New York State as a member of the New England Fishery Management Council
amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act.

topic.name: Civil Rights and Anti-Discrimination Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various aspects of civil rights protections, particularly concerning discrimination based on sexual orientation, gender identity, and other marginalized statuses.

Document 1: no predispute arbitration agreement shall be valid or enforceable if it requires arbitration of...
the validity or enforceability of an agreement to arbitrate shall be determined by a court...
Document 2: prohibits any person in the United States... from being excluded from participation in...
requires the plan proposed by grant applicants... to provide services to persons in a minority category related to sexual orientation or gender identity or expression
Document 5: adopts the definition of 'hate crime'... based on the actual or perceived race, color, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, or disability...
authorizes appropriations to the Department of Justice... for FY2010-FY2012 to prevent and respond to hate crime acts.
Document 6: amends the Fair Housing Act to prohibit discrimination on the basis of sexual orientation or gender identity...
authorizes appropriations to the Department of Housing and Urban Development... for educating the public regarding the protections provided by the amendments made by this Act.
Document 8: prohibits personnel actions from being made on the basis of sexual orientation or gender identity.
Document 10: prohibits an entity that receives federal assistance... from discriminating against prospective adoptive or foster parents solely on the basis of their sexual orientation, gender identification, or marital status.

topic.name: Spectrum Management and Telecommunications Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and regulatory actions related to the allocation, use, and management of electromagnetic spectrum and telecommunications services.

Document 1: Directs the President...to withdraw or modify the assignment to a federal government station of electromagnetic spectrum...
Requires the President...to modify the station's assignment of such spectrum to permit shared federal and non-federal use.
Document 2: Amends the IP-Enabled Voice Communications and Public Safety Act of 1999 to require IP-enabled voice service providers to provide 9-1-1 service...
Requires an annual report to the Committee on Commerce, Science and Transportation of the Senate...detailing the status in each state of the collection and distribution of such fees.
Document 4: Establish a Federal Spectrum Reallocation Commission to make recommendations to the President on the reallocation of federal spectrum...
Requires each federal agency to include a spectrum utilization plan as part of the budget justification documents submitted to Congress each fiscal year.
Document 10: Amends the National Telecommunications and Information Administration Organization Act to require the NTIA to post on its website detailed transition plans from each federal entity...
Requires federal entities to complete spectrum relocation within one year of receiving relocation payments.

topic.name: Consumer Protection and Privacy
topic.description: The documents collectively address various aspects of consumer protection, privacy rights, and the safeguarding of sensitive information across different sectors.

Document 1: temporary fiduciary is needed to protect the benefits of a VA beneficiary
the Secretary shall appoint, to the extent possible, a fiduciary who is a relative
Document 2: directs the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to establish an office within the Bureau of Consumer Protection
disseminate to seniors and their families and caregivers information on the most common fraud schemes
Document 3: prohibits the CFPB from requesting, accessing, collecting, using, retaining, or disclosing nonpublic personal information
the consumer informs the CFPB that such information may be requested
Document 4: allows the release of education records to appropriate persons in an emergency
prohibits deeming the release by an educational institution of education records as a failure to comply
Document 5: establish an Office of Appeals and Redress to implement a redress process
review, adjudicate, and respond in writing, within 30 days, to an individual who files an appeal
Document 6: reaffirms the authority of the Comptroller General to audit and evaluate financial transactions
requires elements of the intelligence community to cooperate with the Comptroller General
Document 7: require federal agencies to make information that is disclosable under such Act available to the public
establish a presumption of openness in administering FOIA
Document 8: requires commercial entities that acquire, maintain, store, or utilize personal information to take reasonable measures to protect
requires a covered entity to notify the Secret Service or the Federal Bureau of Investigation of a security breach
Document 9: requires review by the Supreme Court of appeals of interlocutory or final judgments
expedite appeals related to intelligence activity involving communications authorized by the President
Document 10: authorize commercial applicators of pesticides to create, retain, submit, and convey a pesticide application-related record
satisfy any federal, state, or local law

topic.name: Environmental Legislation and Conservation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at environmental protection, species conservation, and management of natural resources.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to provide incentives for energy production and conservation
increasing tax credits for carbon dioxide sequestration.
Document 2: prohibit the importation or interstate shipment of the red lionfish species.
Document 3: Makes it unlawful to import, export, transport, sell, receive, acquire, possess, or purchase in interstate or foreign commerce plants taken in violation of specified foreign or state law.
Document 4: amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to allow prioritization of petitions to list a species as endangered.
Document 5: requires the Department of Interior to obtain the consent of each state for adding species to the list of threatened or endangered plants and animals.
Document 6: requires the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service to publish a revised recovery plan for the Mexican gray wolf populations.
Document 7: expands access to hunting, fishing, and recreational shooting.
Document 8: prohibit treatment of the Bluefin tuna as an endangered or threatened species.
Document 9: prohibits the shipping, transporting, or donation of horses to be slaughtered for human consumption.
Document 10: requires the Secretary of the Interior to publish complaints in actions related to endangered species determinations.

topic.name: Small Business Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at improving support, financing, and resources for small businesses in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act to direct the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to make financial assistance available to franchisees.
Declares further that a franchisor's authority to repurchase the franchise for noncompliance with its terms and conditions... shall not be considered financial assistance to repay loans.
Document 2: This bill amends the Small Business Act to direct the Small Business Administration (SBA) Office of Women's Business Ownership to address issues concerning specified disciplines required for starting, operating, and increasing a small business.

The mission of the Office is to assist women entrepreneurs in starting, growing, and competing in global markets by providing quality support with access to capital, access to markets, job creation, growth, counseling, and training in a specified manner.
Document 3: Amends the Paperwork Reduction Act to direct agency heads not to impose civil fines for first-time paperwork violations by small business concerns unless there is potential for serious harm to the public interest.

Permits an agency to determine that a fine should not be imposed for a violation that presents a danger to public health or safety if the violation is corrected within 24 hours after receipt by the small business owner of notification of the violation.
Document 4: Amends the Small Business Act to require an offeror with respect to a contract let by a federal agency that is to be awarded pursuant to the negotiated method of procurement that intends to identify a small business as a potential subcontractor...
Provides penalties against offerors failing to provide such notification.
Document 5: Amends the Small Business Act to extend the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) program through FY2022; and the Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) program through FY2023.
Increases, for both the SBIR and STTR programs, the individual small business award levels for program participation at phase one and two levels.
Document 6: Reinstates the authority for Small Business Administration (SBA) low-interest refinancing of small business debt not involving business expansion under the SBA's local development business loan program during any fiscal year in which the cost to the federal government for making loan guarantees under such program is zero.
Repeals a provision of the Small Business Jobs Act of 2010 which terminated such authority as of September 27, 2012.
Document 7: This bill amends the Small Business Act, with respect to the Small Business Administration (SBA) Microloan Program...
The SBA shall compare the operations of a representative sample of eligible intermediaries that participate in the microloan program and of eligible intermediaries that do not.
Document 8: Amends the Small Business Act to direct the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to assign an SBA employee to coordinate SBA programs and activities relating to broadband and emerging information technology (BEIT).
Requires reports from the coordinator to the congressional small business committees on coordinator activities and on federal programs that provide BEIT assistance to small businesses.
Document 9: Establishes the Commission on Women's Business Ownership to review and make recommendations concerning the status of women business owners across the United States.
The role of the federal government in aiding and promoting women business owners.
Document 10: Amends the Federal Credit Union Act to prohibit an insured credit union from making any member business loan that would result in the total amount of such loans outstanding at that credit union at any one time exceeding either: 1.75 times the actual net worth of the credit union, or 12.25% of the total assets of the credit union.
Directs the Government Accountability Office to study the status of member business lending by insured credit unions.

topic.name: Legislative Measures on Federal Oversight
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at federal regulation and oversight in areas such as criminal justice, environmental policies, and public infrastructure.

Document 1: Anti-Gang Enforcement Act of 2009 - Amends the federal criminal code to prohibit certain criminal street gang crimes.
Directs the U.S. Sentencing Commission to amend its sentencing guidelines.
Document 3: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to direct the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants.
Conditions the grant on the grantee's using at least 50% of the grant to fund projects consistent with green building certification programs.
Document 5: This bill authorizes the Department of the Army to produce, treat, manage, and use natural gas located under Fort Knox, Kentucky.
Natural gas produced may only be used to support activities and operations at Fort Knox.
Document 7: Authorizes TransCanada Keystone Pipeline, L.P. to construct, connect, operate, and maintain the pipeline.
Declares that this Act does not alter any federal, state, or local process or condition necessary to secure access from an owner of private property.

topic.name: Election and Voting Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative measures aimed at regulating and improving election processes, voting rights, and military service registration.

Document 1: Prohibits a state that has been redistricted after an apportionment from being redistricted again until after the next apportionment of Representatives
Requires such redistricting to be conducted through a plan developed by the independent redistricting commission established in the state.
Document 2: Amends the Military Selective Service Act to include females between the ages of 18 and 26 within military service registration requirements.
Document 3: Allows a member of the Armed Forces undergoing a permanent change of station and the member's spouse to elect jointly that the spouse may relocate to the new location
Families with other needs may receive exceptions granted by military commanders on a case-by-case basis.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey to the town of Alta, Utah, all interests of the United States in and to specified parcels of National Forest System land
Requires the town to use the land conveyed under this Act for public purposes.
Document 5: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to disclose to the Federal Bureau of Investigation information in the National Directory of New Hires to assist in the enforcement of a federal or state law requiring the registration of sex offenders.
Document 6: Divides the United States into six regions of specified states to provide for interregional primary elections and caucuses
Selection of delegates to political party presidential and vice presidential national nominating conventions.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to apply the ban on contributions and expenditures by foreign nationals in connection with a federal, state, or local election to corporations
Increases the civil monetary penalties applicable to foreign nationals that violate the ban.
Document 8: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 to terminate the Election Assistance Commission
Transfers specified election administration functions of the EAC to the Federal Election Commission.
Document 9: Prohibits any person from knowingly deceiving any other person regarding the time, place, or manner of conducting any federal election
Makes intent to prevent another person from exercising the right to vote an essential element of the offense.
Document 10: Establish the first Saturday and Sunday after the first Friday in November as the days for the election
Amends federal law to establish the same polling place hours in the United States for both congressional and presidential elections.

topic.name: U.S.-Cuba Travel and Trade Policy
topic.description: Legislation concerning the regulation of travel and financial transactions between the United States and Cuba.

Document 1: the President may not prohibit or otherwise regulate travel to or from Cuba by U.S. citizens or legal residents
any regulation in effect on the date of enactment of this Act prohibiting or otherwise regulating such travel or transactions incident to such travel shall cease to have any force or effective
Document 2: prohibits a U.S. person from engaging in any financial transaction with or transfer of funds to the Ministry of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Cuba
Such prohibitions shall not apply to the sale to Cuba of agricultural commodities, medicines, and medical devices
Document 4: Repeals the Radio Broadcasting to Cuba Act and the Television Broadcasting to Cuba Act
eliminating Voice of America: Radio/Television Marti broadcasts to Cuba
Document 6: Prohibits that President from regulating or prohibiting travel to or from Cuba by any U.S. person
transactions incident to travel for the purpose of visiting a close relative who is a national of Cuba
Document 7: Prohibits the President from regulating or prohibiting travel to or from Cuba by U.S. citizens or legal residents
transactions ordinarily incident to such travel relating to accompanied personal baggage, payment of living expenses

topic.name: Native Hawaiian Affairs and Education
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to the rights, education, and governance of Native Hawaiians.

Document 1: Amends the Native Hawaiian Education Act to revise the duties and composition of the Native Hawaiian Education Council.
Requires the Council to hold at least one community consultation each year on each of the islands of Hawaii regarding Native Hawaiian education program grants.
Document 4: Establishes the U.S. Office for Native Hawaiian Relations within the Office of the Secretary of the Interior.
Recognizes the right of the qualified Native Hawaiian constituents to reorganize the single Native Hawaiian governing entity to provide for their common welfare.

topic.name: Women's Health Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at protecting and promoting women's health care rights, including access to abortion, contraception, and related health services.

Document 1: prohibit any recipient of a grant or contract under the family planning program from discriminating against any institutional or individual health care entity on the basis of whether such entity separately provides or refers for abortions
defines 'health care entity' to include an individual physician or other health care professional
Document 2: Amends the Civil Rights Act of 1964 to include lactation as protected conduct
extends the requirement that certain employers provide reasonable break time for an employee to express breast milk
Document 4: prohibit knowingly transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
makes an exception for an abortion necessary to save the life of the minor
Document 6: prohibits any federal funds from being provided to a hospital unless the hospital meets certain conditions related to a woman who is a victim of sexual assault
provides the woman with accurate and unbiased information about emergency contraception
Document 9: require the Director of the Office of Personnel Management to ensure that no multi-state qualified health plan offered in a health benefit exchange provides coverage of abortion
prohibits any state law from preempting the coverage limitations of this Act

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the operations, services, and regulations governing the U.S. Postal Service.

Document 1: Postmark Payment Act of 2014 - Deems any payment required to be made on or before a prescribed date that is delivered by the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) after such date to be received by the payee on the date of the U.S. postmark stamped on the envelope or other cover in which such payment is mailed.

Exempts from this Act any payment: (1) other than a payment on a bill, invoice, or statement of account due; (2) which is required by law, regulation, or contract to be delivered by any method other than by mail; or (3) which is subject to any other provision of federal law specifying how a postmark date shall be used in determining the date on which such payment shall be deemed to have been delivered or made.
Document 2: Amends provisions of federal law relating to Alaska bypass mail transportation to require the U.S. Postal Service (USPS), in selecting a carrier of nonpriority bypass mail to a point in Alaska, to ensure: (1) a minimum of one scheduled flight per week to each nonpriority bypass mail destination with guaranteed capacity for nonpriority bypass mail.
Treats Alaska bypass mail service as a separate competitive product for all purposes and prohibits the transfer of such service to the market-dominant category of mail.
Document 3: Directs the Postmaster General to provide for the issuance of a forever stamp that honors the sacrifices of the members of the U.S. Armed Forces who have been prisoners, missing, or unaccounted for and that depicts the National League of Families POW/MIA flag.
Document 4: This bill prohibits the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) from requiring centralized delivery (whereby mail receptacles of a number of delivery points are grouped or clustered at a single location) for certain residential housing units.
The USPS must establish curbside delivery for such units.
Document 5: Requires the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to issue a semipostal for a five-year period, the proceeds of which shall be transferred to: (1) the National Institutes of Health (NIH) for research into autism spectrum disorders.
The proceeds of which shall be transferred to the Administration on Developmental Disabilities for new demonstration projects that are projects of national significance.
Document 6: Prohibits the U.S. Postal Service from contracting for the delivery of mail on any route with one or more families per mile.
Allows existing contracts to remain in effect until terminated by their terms and to be renewed one or more times.
Document 9: Requires the United States Postal Service, for a period of at least four years, to provide for the issuance and sale of a semipostal in order to increase funding for effective programs targeted at improving permanency outcomes for youth in foster care.
Divides the amount received from such sales equally between programs and activities under the Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment and Adoption Reform Act of 1978.

topic.name: Mining Regulation and Reclamation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and regulations related to the management, reclamation, and environmental safety of mining activities and abandoned mines in the United States.

Document 1: Asset Management Report that describes the Bureau of Reclamation's efforts to maintain infrastructure assets.
standardize and streamline data reporting and processes across regions and areas for the purpose of maintaining such works.
Document 2: Prohibits the issuance of a federal patent for any mining claim unless requirements were fully complied with.
Subjects production of locatable minerals from any mining claim to a royalty of between 2 % and 5%.
Document 3: promulgate regulations for the safe and environmentally responsible reopening of abandoned mines.
require development of emergency response and notification plans before reopening any abandoned mine.
Document 4: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing any proposed regulation under the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act that would adversely impact employment in coal mines.
reduce the quantity of coal available for domestic consumption or for export.
Document 5: Encourages the partial or complete remediation of inactive and abandoned mine sites for the public good by good samaritans.
Sets forth requirements for the issuance of good samaritan discharge permits.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to issue regulations to establish a specified graduated annual production incentive fee.
Requires rentals or royalties received by the United States to be deposited in the Treasury for federal budget deficit reduction.
Document 9: permit the use of moneys in the Abandoned Mine Reclamation Fund to allow transportation and use of dredged materials for abandoned mine reclamation.
Requires application of this Act to permit use of the Fund to allow dredged materials derived from the Delaware River Deepening Project.

topic.name: Federal Commissions and Accountability
topic.description: The documents discuss various federal commissions established to evaluate, enhance, or commemorate aspects of government operations and public service in the United States.

1: Commission on Improving Long-Term Care and Community Services Act of 2010
comprehensive national strategy on how to increase the affordability, accessibility, and effectiveness of long-term care and community services
5: Establishes the Commission on the Accountability and Review of Federal Agencies
evaluate federal agencies and their programs each year for a six-year period
7: Establishes the Grace Commission II to conduct a review of cost control in the federal government
recommendations regarding opportunities for increased efficiency and reduced costs

topic.name: Consumer Safety Regulations
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing consumer safety across multiple industries, including cosmetics, finance, travel, and occupational health.

Document 1: Safe Cosmetics Act of 2010 - Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to expand the regulation of cosmetics
Requires ingredient labeling and disclosure of information on ingredients.
Document 3: Protecting Consumers from Unreasonable Credit Rates Act of 2008 - Amends the Truth in Lending Act to prohibit a creditor from making an extension of credit with an APR exceeding 36%.
Prescribes civil and criminal penalties for violations of this Act.
Document 4: International Travelers Bill of Rights Act of 2009 - Requires site operators to provide information regarding health and safety risks of overseas vacation destinations.
Treats a violation as an unfair or deceptive act or practice prescribed under the Federal Trade Commission Act.
Document 7: ALERT Drivers Act - Requires states to enact laws prohibiting texting while driving to receive federal highway funds.
Imposes minimum penalties for violations of such prohibition.
Document 8: Protecting America's Workers Act - Expands OSHA coverage to government employees and increases protections for whistle blowers.
Prescribes requirements relating to employer's duty to furnish a place of employment free from recognized hazards.

topic.name: Veterans Care and Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving access to healthcare and support services for veterans.

Document 1: modifies the treatment of Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) agreements with service providers to furnish veterans with nursing home care
ensure the safety and quality of care furnished to veterans
Document 2: extends specified Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) programs
Authority for collection of copayments for hospital care and nursing home care is extended through FY2017
Document 3: Grants hiring preferences for federal employment to members of a reserve component of the Armed Forces
Document 4: amends the Energy Employees Occupational Illness Compensation Program Act to include within the meaning of the term 'member of the Special Exposure Cohort'
Document 5: Amends the Civil Rights Act of 1964 to declare that an unlawful employment practice occurs when a discriminatory compensation decision or other practice is adopted
Document 6: Provides for federal-state agreements under which a state will make temporary extended unemployment compensation payments to individuals
Document 7: Amends the Public Health Service Act to state that provisions authorizing the appointment of special consultants or authorizing individual scientists to receive fellowships do not authorize the designation
Document 8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exempt tips from the income tax
Document 9: Amends the Public Works and Economic Development Act to include among the Act's goals encouraging distressed communities to support the formation of business incubators
Document 10: Amends the Federal-State Extended Unemployment Compensation Act of 1970 to revise the federal-state extended unemployment compensation program

topic.name: Legal and Judicial Reforms
topic.description: This cluster addresses various legislative acts aimed at improving legal processes, judicial security, and the handling of specific legal cases in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Attorney General to provide state and local law enforcement agencies information on instituting or assisting investigative searches for alien children believed to be in the United States who are the subject of an application under the Hague Convention.
Amends the International Child Abduction Remedies Act to provide funding, technical assistance, and training to legal providers to assist victims of parental kidnappings.
Document 2: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to require the use of grant funds under the community-oriented policing services program to train and provide security details for prosecutors and judges.
Expands criminal penalties for assaulting or interfering with law enforcement officers and employees.
Document 5: This bill divides the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit into a new Ninth Circuit and a newly established Twelfth Circuit.
The bill designates locations where the new circuits are to hold regular sessions.

topic.name: Veterans and Immigration Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislation related to veterans' benefits and immigration policies affecting military personnel and their families.

Document 1: establish a means by which the judgment of both the President and Congress can be brought to bear when deciding whether the United States should engage in a significant armed conflict
repeals the War Powers Resolution
Document 2: extends from two years to five years following discharge or release the eligibility period for certain veterans who served in combat after the Persian Gulf War to receive hospital care
notwithstanding a lack of evidence to conclude that their condition is attributable to such service
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include a 'permanent partner' within the scope of such Act
defines a 'permanent partner' as an individual 18 or older who is in a committed, intimate relationship
Document 4: exempt from worldwide or numerical limitations on immigrant visas the sons and daughters of Filipino World War II veterans
who were naturalized under the Immigration Act of 1990
Document 5: permits a veteran with any compensable service-connected disability
to use commissary and exchange stores on the same basis as a member of the Armed Forces
Document 6: establishes the Commission on Wartime Relocation and Internment of Latin Americans of Japanese descent
to investigate U.S. relocation, internment, and deportation
Document 7: establishes in the Treasury the Merchant Mariner Equity Compensation Fund for payments by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs
to each individual who was a documented member of the U.S. merchant marine
Document 8: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to eliminate the per country numerical limitation for employment-based immigrants
increase the per country numerical limitation for family based immigrants from 7% to 15%
Document 9: permits a veteran with any compensable service-connected disability
to use commissary and exchange stores on the same basis as a member of the Armed Forces
Document 10: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to set forth provisions regarding the expedited removal of inadmissible arriving aliens
criminal aliens

topic.name: Consumer Safety and Health Regulations
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving safety standards and health regulations for various consumer products and public health issues.

Document 1: establishing, disseminating, and ensuring the implementation by schools of concussion management guidelines
funding implementation by schools of computerized preseason baseline and post-injury neuropsychological testing for student athletes.
Document 2: requires annual baseline concussion testing prior to a student athlete's participation in contact sports
establishes the Presidential Commission on Intercollegiate Athletics to review, analyze, and report on health and safety protections for student athletes.
Document 4: establish the Epidemiology and Laboratory Capacity Grant Program to assist public health agencies in improving surveillance for infectious diseases
provide for the expansion of applied epidemiology training programs.
Document 5: requires the Consumer Product Safety Commission to promulgate a final consumer product safety standard for free-standing clothing storage units
to protect children from tip-over-related death or injury.
Document 9: requires nongovernmental, independent third party testing of children's products
increases the actions the CPSC may take in ordering recalls of products presenting substantial hazards.

topic.name: Climate Change Policy and Education
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures and programs aimed at addressing climate change through education, research, and national security implications.

Document 1: the evidence for human-induced climate change is overwhelming and undeniable
establish a Climate Change Education Program
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to submit to Congress a National Intelligence Estimate on the anticipated geopolitical effects of global climate change
the projected impact on the military installations, capabilities, and operations of the effects of global climate change
Document 3: work in the National Marine Sanctuary System, coral reefs, and other coastal, estuarine, and marine habitats
natural and cultural resources conservation projects
Document 4: establish within the Office of Oceanic and Atmospheric Research of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration
conduct scientific research on abrupt climate change
Document 6: require the program of marine and hydrokinetic renewable energy technology research
develop incentives for industry to comply with such standards
Document 7: establish a program to strengthen federal activities for ensuring that commercially distributed seafood meets federal food quality and safety requirements
increase the number of laboratories certified to Food and Drug Administration (FDA) standards
Document 8: maintain and operate at least one Doppler weather radar site within 55 miles of each city in the United States
ensure that each such radar conforms with the same standards that apply to radars operated by Commerce

topic.name: Feasibility Studies and Assessments
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various feasibility studies and assessments related to environmental, housing, historical, and infrastructure projects in the United States.

1: feasibility study of alternatives to augment water supplies
2: financial assistance to develop community-based housing organizations
technical assistance for community development projects
3: special resource study of Point Peter
evaluate the national significance of Point Peter
4: revise the prohibition against the President's designation
factors in determining beneficiary developing countries
5: expands the boundaries of the Cibola National Forest
6: conduct a special resource study of the Colonel Charles Young Home
determine the suitability and feasibility of designating the Home as a unit of the National Park System
7: study motor vehicle refueling assistance to qualified individuals
report to Congress the study results
8: study of establishing a deep water sea port in the Arctic
report to Congress on feasibility and potential
9: quantitative analysis of domestic petroleum coke production
10: contract with the Institute of Medicine for expanded study

topic.name: Terrorism Legislation and Victim Support
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at addressing issues related to terrorism, including victim compensation, financial sanctions, and the designation of state sponsors of terrorism.

Document 1: amends the Terrorism Risk Insurance Act of 2002
to satisfy a judgment based upon an act of terrorism
Document 2: provides permanent resident status adjustment for families affected by the September 11 attacks
deemed to be a beneficiary of the September 11th Victim Compensation Fund
Document 4: directs the Department of State to submit a determination regarding Pakistan's support for international terrorism
Document 7: amends the Defense Base Act to require death benefits for individuals who die as a result of an act of terrorism
Document 9: expresses the sense of Congress regarding curtailing financial resources of ISIL
the President may designate a country as a Jurisdiction of Terrorism Financing Concern

topic.name: HIV/AIDS and Health Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at addressing the needs and rights of individuals living with HIV/AIDS and related health issues.

Document 1: Housing Opportunities for Persons With AIDS Modernization Act of 2016
revised the formula and requirements for distributing funds under the Housing Opportunities for Persons With Aids (HOPWA) Program.
Document 2: Housing for Persons With AIDS Modernization Act of 2014
revise the formula and terms for allocations of grants to states, local governments, and nonprofit organizations for housing programs for persons with acquired immune deficiency syndrome (AIDS).
Document 9: Ending the HIV/AIDS Epidemic Act of 2013
establish an integrated, five-year Global HIV/AIDS-Free Generation Strategy to expand efforts to combat global HIV/AIDS.

topic.name: Insurance and Financial Regulation
topic.description: The documents focus on various aspects of insurance and financial regulations affecting different sectors, including nonadmitted insurance, healthcare, and consumer protection.

Document 1: Prohibits any state other than the home state of an insured from requiring a premium tax payment for nonadmitted insurance.
Subjects nonadmitted insurance solely to the regulatory requirements of the insured's home state.
Document 4: Establishes the Office of Paid Family and Medical Leave within the Social Security Administration.
Entitles every individual to a family and medical leave insurance (FMLI) benefit payment.
Document 5: Treats independent pharmacies negotiating contract terms with a health plan for the provision of health care items or services the same under the antitrust laws as an employee engaged in concerted activities.
Provides that this Act does not exempt from application of antitrust laws any agreement or unlawful conspiracy that...
Document 6: Requires an insurance issuer that offers a long term care insurance plan to implement claims dispute resolution procedures.
Allows an enrollee to appeal the decision of an independent reviewer to an appropriate state court.
Document 9: Prohibits... any person from being a party to any agreement resolving or settling a patent infringement claim.
Authorizes the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to exempt agreements in furtherance of market competition and for the benefit of consumers.

topic.name: Public Health and Safety Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation aimed at improving public health, safety, and welfare through various measures including disease prevention, environmental protection, and financial regulations.

1: Requires the Attorney General to direct the Bureau of Prisons to allow community organizations to distribute sexual barrier protection devices
Develop and implement a five-year strategy to reduce the prevalence and spread of STIs in such facilities.
2: Amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to revise and expand requirements for assessment by covered water systems of their vulnerability to a range of intentional acts

Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to promulgate regulations to establish increasingly stringent, risk-based performance standards for the security of such systems.
3: Requires the mortgagee of a residential mortgage to file a specified notice of default before foreclosure
Prescribes loan modification and workout plan duties for loan servicers.
4: Amends the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act to eliminate sequential and cumulative adjustments for inflation
Prohibits adjustments for inflation or any other factor.
5: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to prohibit a person from extending any consumer credit through the use by the consumer of a check or other negotiable instrument
Shields a consumer from liability for the principal amount of a negotiable instrument sent in violation of this Act.
6: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to submit annually to Congress a future-years veterans program
Requires each program to set forth a five-year VA plan to address the U.S. commitment to veterans.
7: Adjusts the boundary of the Arapaho National Forest in Colorado to incorporate approximately 92.95 acres of land
Requires the USDA to include all federal land within the boundary in the Bowen Gulch Protection Area.
8: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to authorize the governor of Virginia to petition for natural gas exploration
Requires deposit of 50% into a special account in the Treasury to mitigate for any environmental damage.
9: Establishes the Gulf Coast Ecosystem Restoration Task Force to consult and provide recommendations regarding restoration plans
Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to deposit into the Fund sums equal to penalties related to the Deepwater Horizon explosion.
10: Amends the Unfunded Mandates Reform Act to require regulatory impact analyses for rules that do not involve a legislative mandate
Require federal agencies to prepare and publish an initial and final regulatory impact analysis prior to promulgating any proposed or final rule.

topic.name: Financial and Regulatory Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at regulating financial institutions, consumer protection, and specific industry practices.

Document 1: Amends the Truth in Lending Act (TILA) with respect to minimum standards for residential mortgage loans
Directs the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) to exempt a creditor with assets of less than $10 billion
Document 8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow bonds guaranteed by any federal home loan bank to be treated as tax-exempt state or local bonds
Document 10: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a five-year carryback of net operating losses
Denies such extended loss carryover period to certain taxpayers

topic.name: Legal Reforms and Consumer Protection
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various legal reforms aimed at enhancing consumer rights, transparency, and protections in different sectors including healthcare, financial institutions, and media.

Document 1: Authorizes the presiding judge of a U.S. appellate court or U.S. district court to permit the photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising to the public of court proceedings
Prohibits any audio pickup or broadcast of conferences which occur in a court proceeding between attorneys and their clients
Document 2: Requires retail distributors of television receiving equipment to place adjacent to each unit displayed for sale a consumer alert
Imposes similar requirements on sellers that use direct mail, catalog, or electronic means such as the Internet
Document 3: Grants a financial institution the right to appeal a material supervisory determination contained in a final report of examination
Establishes in the Federal Financial Institutions Examination Council the Office of Independent Examination Review
Document 4: The FDA is prohibited from requiring the disclosure, collection, or reporting of certain information concerning such drugs or devices

Except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct, any person who manufactures, imports, distributes, prescribes, or administers an investigational drug or device is not liable under state or federal law
Document 5: Directs the Attorney General to make grants to states and local governments for the complete and accurate recording, by both audio and video means, of every custodial interrogation occurring within the state or unit of local government
Document 6: Allows any U.S. person against whom a lawsuit for defamation is brought in a foreign country to bring an action in a U.S. district court

Allows the award of treble damages if it is determined by a preponderance of the evidence that the person or entity bringing the foreign lawsuit intentionally engaged in a scheme to suppress rights under the First Amendment
Document 7: Requires the Privacy and Civil Liberties Oversight Board to appoint attorneys to serve as public interest advocates in proceedings before the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Court
Permits the FISC to request review
Document 8: Prescribes security procedures which an entity that maintains or communicates sensitive account or personal information must implement and enforce
Grants exclusive enforcement powers to specified federal regulatory agencies with oversight of financial institutions
Document 9: Specifies that an unlawful employment practice is established when the complaining party demonstrates that age or participation in investigations was a motivating factor
Declares that a complaining party shall not be required to demonstrate that age or retaliation was the sole cause of a practice
Document 10: This bill amends title XVIII of the Social Security Act to expand Medicare coverage of costs associated with clinical trials
A trial of a medical device shall, for purposes of Medicare coverage, be deemed to meet the definition of a category A or category B clinical trial

topic.name: Federal Land and Resource Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses the management, transfer, and conservation of federal lands and resources, including provisions for environmental protection and infrastructure development.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey specified federal land for land conservation and related research.
Contingent on the District's agreeing to use the property for land conservation activities.
