Document 2: Provides for the transfer of amounts derived from oil and gas development leases to Colorado for conservation and restoration.
Used for capital improvement projects to address impacts of oil or gas development.
Document 4: Directs the Administrator of General Services to convey property for the National Women's History Museum.
Requires terms to address mitigation of developmental impacts to existing federal buildings.

topic.name: Healthcare and Public Safety Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative acts related to healthcare access, public health workforce development, and public safety enhancements.

Document 1: Medicare Oral Health Rehabilitative Enhancement Act of 2009
permits physical therapy to be furnished under the Medicare program
Document 5: Public Health Workforce Development Act of 2009
establish the Public Health Workforce Scholarship Program to assure an adequate supply of public health professionals
Document 9: Nursing Education and Quality of Health Care Act of 2007
require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants to educate nurses to serve in rural areas

topic.name: U.S. Border and Environmental Regulations
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation related to border security, environmental protection, and the management of natural resources in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to hire, train, and assign additional CBP officers at U.S. land ports of entry.
establish a program for awarding grants for the purchase of identification and detection equipment for law enforcement officers.
Document 2: Amends the Nonindigenous Aquatic Nuisance Prevention and Control Act to prevent the introduction and spread of aquatic nuisance species.
Requires vessel operators to conduct ballast water management operations in accordance with an approved ballast water management plan.
Document 3: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to provide penalties for conspiracy to possess or traffic in controlled substances outside the United States.
Document 4: Requires new U.S. vessels to comply with regulations requiring double hulls to prevent oil spills.
Directs the Secretary to continue individual port and waterway safety assessments.

topic.name: Federal Legislative Acts
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various federal legislative acts addressing oversight, accountability, and specific provisions for different sectors, including child protection, economic stability, land management, and health services.

Document 1: International Megan's Law of 2010 - protection of children from sexual exploitation
monitoring the international travel of sex traffickers
Document 2: Special Inspector General for the Troubled Asset Relief Program Act of 2009
authority to conduct, supervise, and coordinate an audit or investigation
Document 3: Land Management Workforce Flexibility Act of 2015
eligible to compete for a permanent appointment in the agency
Document 4: Secretary of Veterans Affairs to appoint licensed hearing aid specialists
annual report on timely access of veterans to hearing health services
Document 5: Amends the National Security Act of 1947
report such request to the Attorney General and the congressional intelligence committees
Document 6: Death in Custody Reporting Act of 2008
requires states to report on the death of any person who is detained
Document 7: Government Accountability Office Act of 2008
revises certain administrative requirements for the Government Accountability Office
Document 8: Fiscal Responsibility and Retirement Security Act
requires congressional approval of the designation by the Secretary of Health and Human Services
Document 9: Huna Tlingit Traditional Gull Egg Use Act
authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to allow members of the Hoonah Indian Association to collect the eggs
Document 10: Federal Agency Program Realignment and Closure Act
establishes the Federal Agency Program Realignment and Closure Commission

topic.name: Energy and Environmental Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to energy production, environmental conservation, and sustainable infrastructure development in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act (OCSLA) to prescribe procedures for petition, by the governor of a state with a new producing area within the offshore administrative boundaries beyond the state's submerged land, to make the new producing area available for oil and gas leasing.
Directs the Secretary of Energy to expand and accelerate research and development efforts for advanced batteries.
Document 2: This bill revises the process for federal approval of major infrastructure projects by establishing best practices, requiring coordination of federal agency review of projects, and by shortening the period for challenges to final decisions for issuing project permits.
involves renewable or conventional energy production, electricity transmission, surface transportation, aviation, ports and waterways, water resource projects.
Document 3: This bill amends the Clean Air Act to modify provisions regarding geographical areas that do not attain the national ambient air quality standards (NAAQS) for ozone and carbon monoxide.
States must exclude from the nonattainment area all counties within each statistical area that are not in violation of the NAAQS concerned.
Document 4: Establishes in the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) an Office of Sustainable Housing and Communities (OSHC).
Requires the use of a comprehensive planning grant to carry out a project to coordinate land use, housing, transportation, and infrastructure planning processes across jurisdictions and agencies.
Document 5: Amends the Capper-Cramton Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior, acting through the Director of the National Park Service (NPS), to make grants to Maryland, Virginia, West Virginia, and the District of Columbia for assistance in acquiring lands and interests therein that affect or are within the National Capital region.
for parks; open space; green space corridors that link public lands.
Document 8: Amends the Lower Rio Grande Valley Water Resources Conservation and Improvement Act of 2000 to authorize specified additional projects, including projects for water conservation and improvement.
Permits each project that the Secretary of the Interior conducts or participates in to include: the replacement of irrigation canals and lateral canals with buried pipelines.
